message: Add changelog for Check Point syslog parsing

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+---
+title: Check Point syslog structured-data dialect parsing
+type: feature
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - codex
+pr: 5851
+created: 2026-03-02T10:22:49.136178Z
+---
+
+`parse_syslog()` and `read_syslog` now accept common Check Point structured-data variants that are not strictly RFC 5424 compliant. This includes `key:"value"` parameters, semicolon-separated parameters, and records that omit an SD-ID entirely.
+
+For records without an SD-ID, Tenzir now normalizes the structured data under `checkpoint@2620`, so downstream pipelines can use a stable field path.
+
+For example, the message `<134>1 ... - [action:"Accept"; conn_direction:"Incoming"]` now parses successfully and maps to `structured_data["checkpoint@2620"]`. This improves interoperability with Check Point exports and reduces ingestion-time preprocessing.
